question_text: Which of the following is the correct definition of a moment?

options.label: A
options.text: The turning effect of a force around a pivot, calculated as force divided by distance

options.label: B
options.text: The turning effect of a force around a pivot, calculated as force multiplied by perpendicular distance

options.label: C
options.text: The weight of an object multiplied by its height above the ground

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which a lever rotates around its pivot point

explanation: A moment is the turning effect produced by a force acting around a pivot point. It is calculated by multiplying the force by the perpendicular distance from the pivot. The larger the distance or force, the greater the turning effect (moment).
